memoryArtifacts.headline: James Tomlinson - Ancestry of Larry Joe Hiatt
memoryArtifacts.description:
Born in 1801, in Fayette County, Kentucky; the son of William Tomlinson.

At the age of eleven, James moved with his family to Maury County, Tennessee, settling in the Culleoka area.

James married fi …

zh.Virginia, British Colonial America: 布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州
zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary Jane Page於1630年6月25日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州易斯爾 本 懷特易斯爾 本 懷特時，她的父親Thomas Page25歲，母親Elizabeth Agnes Allen23歲。她於大約1650年與Valentine Richard Allen在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州劍橋大學默里 肯特聖 比特也斯 帕里什結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1694年6月11日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州笛卡爾 拉帕韓諾可享年63歲，埋葬在美國維吉尼亞州劍橋大學默里 肯特普特尼斯 密爾聖 彼得斯 艾比斯可帕爾 邱奇 其特利。

zh-hans.Virginia, British Colonial America: 布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mary Jane Page于1630年6月25日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州易斯爾 本 懷特易斯爾 本 懷特时，她的父亲Thomas Page25岁，母亲Elizabeth Agnes Allen23岁。她于大约1650年与Valentine Richard Allen在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州劍橋大學默里 肯特聖 比特也斯 帕里什结婚。他们至少是9个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她去世于1694年6月11日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州笛卡爾 拉帕韓諾可享年63岁，埋葬在美国弗吉尼亚州劍橋大學默里 肯特普特尼斯 密爾聖 彼得斯 艾比斯可帕爾 邱奇 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Page
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and French: occupational or status name for a young servant, Middle English page, paige, Old French page (from Italian paggio, ultimately from Greek paidion, a diminutive of pais ‘boy, child’). The surname has also been established in Ireland since the 16th century. In North America, this surname is also a shortened form of the French cognate Lepage .
French Canadian (Pagé): altered form of French Paget , a diminutive of 1. Compare Pashia .
North German: metonymic occupational name for a horse dealer, from Middle Low German page ‘horse’.

variants.name: Pages
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Mario, Eduardo, Jorge, Luis, Mauricio, Ramon, Fernando, Jose, Lazaro, Pedro, Alfredo.  French Jacques, Lucien, Michel.

Catalan and southern French (Pagès): occupational name for a peasant farmer or agricultural laborer, from Catalan and Old Occitan pagès ‘peasant’ (from Late Latin pagensis, a derivative of pagus ‘village, country district’).
English: variant of Page with genitival of post-medieval excrescent s.

variants.name: Paget
meanings: French: diminutive of   Page  .
English: variant of   Padgett  .

variants.name: Paige
meanings: English (mainly Devon): variant of   Page  .

variants.name: Pageau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Stephane, Alphonse, Andre, Fernand, Gisele, Jean Claude, Lucien, Philippe.
French: diminutive of   Page  .

variants.name: Lepage
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Fernand, Pierre, Raoul, Adelard, Alcide, Christophe, Lucien, Normand, Adrien.
French: occupational or status name for a young servant, a variant of Page 1, with fused masculine definite article le. Compare Lapage .
French Canadian: altered form of Pagé (see Page 2).

variants.name: Padgett
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire): from a Middle English diminutive of page, paige ‘servant, groom’ (see Page ).

variants.name: Littlepage
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English littel ‘small’ +Middle English, Old French page ‘young servant’ (see Page ).

variants.name: Pashia
meanings: Americanized form of French Canadian Pagé (see Page 2).
History: It was Jean Baptiste Pagé from QC whose surname was changed to Pashia after he settled in MO in the 1810s. Some of his descendants bear a rare variant, Pashea.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Jane Page was born on 25 June 1630, in Isle of Wight, Isle of Wight, Virginia, British Colonial America, her father, Thomas Page, was 25 and her mother, Elizabeth Agnes Allen, was 23. She married Valentine Richard Allen about 1650, in St. Peter's Parish, New Kent, Virginia, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 7 daughters. She died on 11 June 1694, in Old Rappahannock, Virginia, British Colonial America, at the age of 63, and was buried in Saint Peters Episcopal Church Cemetery, Putneys Mill, New Kent, Virginia, United States.

Elle a épousé Valentine Richard Allen environ 1650, à St. Peter's Parish, New Kent, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 9 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée le 11 juin 1694, à Old Rappahannock, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterrée à Saint Peters Episcopal Church Cemetery, Putneys Mill, New Kent, Virginie, États-Unis.

ja.lifeSummary: Mary Jane Pageが1630年6月25日にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州イスル オフ ウィトイスル オフ ウィトで生まれた時，父Thomas Pageは25歳，母Elizabeth Agnes Allenは23歳であった。約1650年にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州ニュー ケントスト. ペテル'ス パーイッシュでValentine Richard Allenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも9人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1694年6月11日にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州オルド ラッパハノックで63歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国バージニア州ニュー ケントプウトニーズ ミルサイント ペテルズ エパスコプアル チャーチ セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mary Jane Page(이)가 1630년 6월 25일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 이슬일 프 와이트 이슬일 프 와이트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Page(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Agnes Allen(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 대략 1650년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 뉴 켄트 스트. 피터'스 파리시에서 Valentine Richard Allen(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1694년 6월 11일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 드 랩아아노크에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 버지니아 주 뉴 켄트 풋니스 미 세인트 페테르스 프이스코팔 처르치 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Jane Page nasceu em 25 de junho de 1630, em Isle of Wight, Isle of Wight, Virginia, British Colonial America, seu pai, Thomas Page, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Agnes Allen, tinha 23 anos. ru.St. Peter's Parish, New Kent, Virginia,: ст. питир'с пэриш, ню кэнт, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mary Jane Page родилась 25 июня 1630 г. в местности исл оф ваит, исл оф ваит, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика, её отцу по имени Thomas Page было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Agnes Allen, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Valentine Richard Allen около 1650 г. в местности ст. питир'с пэриш, ню кэнт, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Она умерла 11 июня 1694 г. в местности олд раппаханнокк, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 63 лет и была похоронена в местности сеинт питирс эпископаль чурч кеметири, путнис милл, ню кэнт, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mary Jane Page nació el 25 de junio de 1630, en Isle of Wight, Isle of Wight, Virginia, British Colonial America, su papá, Thomas Page, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Agnes Allen, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Valentine Richard Allen aproximadamente 1650, en St. Peter's Parish, New Kent, Virginia, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 9 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 11 de junio de 1694, en Old Rappahannock, Virginia, British Colonial America, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultada en Saint Peters Episcopal Church Cemetery, Putneys Mill, New Kent, Virginia, Estados Unidos.
